21.32: but if the ox strikes a male or female servant, thirty shekels of silver shall be paid to the master of the slave and the ox shall be stoned.
21.33: If a man opens a cistern, or if a man digs a cistern and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,
21.34: The owner of the cistern shall compensate: he shall pay back to the owner the value of the animal in silver, and the animal killed shall be his.
21.35: If one man's ox gores another man's ox and it dies, they shall sell the live ox and divide the proceeds between them; they shall also divide the ox that is killed.
21.36: But if it is found that the ox had been butting with its horn for a long time and that its owner did not supervise it, the latter shall compensate by giving ox for ox and the ox killed shall be his.
21.37: If a man steals an ox or a lamb and slaughters it or sells it, he shall restore five oxen for the ox and four lambs for the lamb.

22.1: If a thief is caught breaking in at night and is beaten and dies, we are not responsible for his bloodshed.,
22.2: But if the sun has risen, we will be responsible for his bloodshed. The thief will make restitution: if he has nothing, he will be sold for what he stole.
22.3: If what he stole, whether ox, donkey or sheep, is still alive in his possession, he will return double the amount.
22.4: If a man causes damage in a field or vineyard by letting his livestock graze on another's field, he shall give as compensation the best of his field and the best of his vineyard.
22.5: If a fire breaks out and, after reaching the thorns, consumes sheaves, or standing wheat, or a field, the one who started the fire shall give compensation.
22.6: If a man entrusts money or objects to another for safekeeping and they are stolen from the latter's house, the thief, if found, will return double the amount.
22.7: If the thief is not found, the master of the house will appear before God to declare whether he has not laid hands on his neighbor's property.
22.8: Whatever the object of the offense, whether ox, donkey, sheep, garment, or any lost item, about which it is said, "That's right." The case of both parties will go to God, and the one whom God has condemned will restore double to his neighbor.
22.9: If a man entrusts to another man an ox, a sheep, or any head of cattle, and the animal dies, breaks a limb, or is stolen, without there being a witness,
22.10: The Lord's oath will be taken between the two parties, so that it may be known whether the custodian has not taken possession of his neighbor's property, and the owner of the beast will accept this oath and the other will not have to compensate.
22.11: But if the animal was stolen from his home, he will have to compensate the owner.
22.12: If it was torn apart by a ferocious beast, he shall produce the remains as evidence and he shall not have to compensate for the torn beast.
22.13: If a man borrows an animal from another and it breaks a limb or dies, while its owner is not present, compensation will be due.
22.14: If the owner is present, no compensation will be given. If the animal was rented, the rental price will serve as compensation.
22.15: If a man seduces a virgin who is not betrothed and sleeps with her, he will pay her dowry and take her as his wife.
22.16: If the father refuses to grant it to him, the seducer will pay the money given for the dowry of virgins.
22.17: You will not let the sorceress live.
22.18: Anyone who has dealings with an animal shall be put to death.
22.19: He who offers sacrifices to the gods and not to the Lord alone will be condemned.
22.20: You shall not mistreat the stranger or oppress him, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt.
22.21: You shall not grieve the widow or the orphan.
22.22: If you afflict them, they will cry out to me, and I will hear their cry.,
22.23: My anger will blaze forth, and I will destroy you with the sword; your wives will become widows, and your children orphans.
22.24: If you lend money to one of my people, to the poor among you, you shall not be to him as a creditor, you shall not demand interest from him.
22.25: If you take your neighbor's cloak as a pledge, you must return it to him before sunset.,
22.26: for it is his only covering, it is the garment with which he wraps his body: on what else would he sleep? If he cries out to me, I will hear him because I am compassionate.
22.27: You shall not blaspheme against God, nor curse a prince of your people.
22.28: You will not delay to offer me the firstfruits of your harvest and of your winepress. You will give me the firstborn of your sons.
22.29: You shall do the same with the firstborn of your cow and of your sheep: it shall remain seven days with its mother and on the eighth day you shall give it to me.
22.30: You shall be holy men to me; you shall not eat the torn flesh that is found in the fields; you shall throw it to the dogs.

23.1: You shall not spread false rumors, you shall not give your hand to a wicked man by serving as an incriminating witness.
23.2: You will not follow the multitude to do evil, nor will you testify in a lawsuit by siding with the majority to sway justice.
23.3: You will not favor a weak person in his trial either.
23.4: If you come across your enemy's ox or his stray donkey, you will not fail to bring it back to him.
23.5: If you see the donkey of the one who hates you succumbing under its load, you will beware of abandoning it, join your efforts with his to unload it.
23.6: You will not sway the rights of the poor in their lawsuit.
23.7: You will turn away from a false cause and you will not put the innocent and the just to death, for I will not absolve a guilty person.
23.8: You shall not accept gifts, for gifts blind the discerning and ruin just causes.
23.9: You shall not oppress the stranger; you know how a stranger feels, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt.
23.10: For six years you will sow your land and reap its produce.
23.11: But the seventh one you shall leave and abandon, and the poor of your people shall eat them, and the beasts of the field shall eat what remains. You shall do the same with your vineyards and olive groves.
23.12: For six days you shall do your work, but on the seventh day you shall rest, so that your ox and your donkey may have rest, and the son of your servant and the stranger may breathe.
23.13: You will take heed to all that I have told you; you will not pronounce the name of foreign gods, nor will any of their names be heard from your mouth.
23.14: Three times each year you will celebrate a festival in my honor.
23.15: You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread: for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in that month you came out of Egypt, and no one shall appear before me empty-handed.
23.16: You shall observe the Feast of Harvest, of the firstfruits of your labor, of what you have sown in the fields, and the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year, when you gather in the fruit of your labor from the fields.
23.17: Three times a year, all your males shall appear before the Lord God.
23.18: You shall not offer with leavened bread the blood of my sacrifice, nor shall the fat of my feast be kept overnight until morning.
23.19: You shall bring the firstfruits of your land to the house of the Lord your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
23.20: Behold, I send an angel before you, to guard you on the way and to bring you to the place that I have prepared.
23.21: Be on your guard in his presence and listen to his voice; do not resist him, for he would not forgive your transgression, because my name is in him.
23.22: But if you listen to his voice and do everything I say, I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.
23.23: For my angel will go before you and lead you to the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Canaanites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, and I will destroy them.
23.24: You shall not worship their gods and you shall not serve them, you shall not imitate their practices, but you shall overthrow and break their stelae.
23.25: You shall serve the Lord your God, and he will bless your bread and your water, and I will take sickness away from among you.
23.26: In your land there will be no woman who loses her fruit, nor a barren woman; I will fill the number of your days.
23.27: I will send my terror before you, I will throw into confusion all the peoples among whom you come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs before you.
23.28: I will send hornets before you, which will drive out the Hivites, the Canaanites, and the Hittites from your presence.
23.29: I will not drive them out of your presence in a single year, lest the land become a desert and the wild beasts multiply against you.
23.30: I will drive them out little by little from before you, until you increase in number and can occupy the land.
23.31: I will establish your boundaries from the Red Sea to the Sea of the Philistines and from the desert to the river, for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hands and you will drive them out from before you.
23.32: You shall not make an alliance with them, nor with their gods.
23.33: They will not live in your land, lest they cause you to sin against me; you would serve their gods, and that would be a snare for you.»

24.1: God said to Moses, «Come up to the Lord, you and Aaron, Nadab and Abiu and seventy of the elders of Israel, and worship from a distance.
24.2: Moses alone will approach the Lord; the others will not approach, and the people will not go up with him.»
24.3: Moses came and told the people all the words of the Lord and all the laws, and the whole people responded with one voice: «All the words that the Lord has spoken, we will fulfill.»
24.4: Moses wrote down all the words of the Lord. Then, rising early in the morning, he built an altar at the foot of the mountain and erected twelve pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.
24.5: He sent young men, children of Israel, and they offered burnt offerings to the Lord and sacrificed bulls as peace offerings.
24.6: Moses took half of the blood and put it in basins, and he sprinkled the other half on the altar.
24.7: Having taken the book of the covenant, he read it in the presence of the people, who responded, "All that the Lord has said, we will do and obey."«
24.8: Moses took the blood and sprinkled it on the people, saying, «This is the blood of the covenant that the Lord has made with you in accordance with all these words.»
24.9: Moses went up with Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel,
24.10: and they saw the God of Israel: under his feet was something like a work of brilliant sapphires, as clear as the sky itself.
24.11: And he did not lay his hand on the chosen ones of the children of Israel: they saw God and they ate and drank.
24.12: The Lord said to Moses, «Come up to me on the mountain and stay there, and I will give you the tablets of stone with the law and the commandments I have written for their instruction.»
24.13: Moses arose, with Joshua his servant, and Moses went up to the mountain of God.
24.14: He said to the elders, »Wait here for us until we return to you. Aaron and Hur will be with you; if anyone has a dispute, let them go to them.”
24.15: Moses went up to the mountain and the cloud covered the mountain,
24.16: The glory of the Lord rested on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it for six days. On the seventh day, the Lord called to Moses from within the cloud.
24.17: The appearance of the glory of the Lord was, in the sight of the children of Israel, like a devouring fire on the top of the mountain.
24.18: Moses entered the midst of the cloud and went up the mountain, and Moses remained on the mountain forty days and forty nights.

25.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
25.2: «Tell the children of Israel to take up an offering for me; from every man who gives it willingly you shall receive the offering for me.
25.3: This is the offering you will receive from them: gold, silver, and bronze,
25.4: of violet purple, scarlet purple, crimson, fine linen, and goat hair,
25.5: Ramskins dyed red, dolphin skins, and acacia wood,
25.6: oil for the lampstand, spices for the anointing oil and for the incense,
25.7: onyx stones and other stones to be set for the ephod and the pectoral.
25.8: They will make me a sanctuary, and I will dwell among them.
25.9: You will follow everything I am going to show you, the design of the Tabernacle and the design of all its furnishings.»
25.10: «"They shall make an ark of acacia wood, its length shall be two and a half cubits, its width one and a half cubits, and its height one and a half cubits.
25.11: You shall cover it with pure gold, inside and out, and you shall make a wreath of gold all around it.
25.12: You shall cast four gold rings for her, and put them on her four feet, two rings on one side and two rings on the other.
25.13: You shall make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
25.14: You will pass the bars through the rings on the sides of the ark, so that they can be used to carry the ark.
25.15: The bars will remain in the rings of the ark and will not be removed.
25.16: You shall put into the ark the testimony that I will give you.
25.17: You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold, its length shall be two and a half cubits and its width one and a half cubits.
25.18: You shall make two cherubim of gold, you shall make them of hammered gold, at the two ends of the mercy seat.
25.19: Make a cherub at one end and a cherub at the other end; you shall make the cherubim coming out of the mercy seat at its two ends.
25.20: The cherubim shall have their wings spread upwards, covering the mercy seat with their wings and facing each other, the faces of the cherubim shall be turned towards the mercy seat.
25.21: You shall place the mercy seat on top of the ark, and you shall put in the ark the testimony that I will give you.
25.22: There I will meet with you and I will give you, from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim that are on the ark of the testimony, all the commands that I will give you for the children of Israel.
25.23: You shall make a table of acacia wood, its length shall be two cubits, its width one cubit, and its height one and a half cubits.
25.24: You shall overlay it with pure gold and put a gold wreath all around it.
25.25: You shall make around it a frame of one palm and you shall make a garland of gold on the frame, all around.
25.26: You shall make four gold rings for the table and put the rings on its four corners, which shall be at its four feet.
25.27: The rings will be near the frame, to receive the bars that will support the table.
25.28: You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold; they shall be used to carry the table.
25.29: You shall make its dishes, its censers, its bowls and its cups for libations; you shall make them of pure gold.
25.30: You shall place the loaves of the Presence on the table, continually before my face.
25.31: You shall make a lampstand of pure gold; the lampstand, its base and its shaft, shall be made of hammered gold; its cups, its buds and its flowers shall be of one piece.
25.32: Six branches will come out of its sides, three branches from the lampstand on one side and three branches from the lampstand on the other side.
25.33: On the first branch there will be three almond blossom calyxes, bud and flower, and on the second branch three almond blossom calyxes, bud and flower; the same will be true for the six branches coming from the candelabra.
25.34: On the stem of the candlestick, there will be four almond blossom calyxes, their buds and their flowers.
25.35: There will be a button under the first two branches coming from the stem of the candlestick, a button under the next two branches coming from the stem of the candlestick, and a button under the last two branches coming from the stem of the candlestick, according to the six branches coming out of the stem of the candlestick.
25.36: These buttons and branches will be of the same piece as the candlestick, the whole thing will be a mass of beaten gold, of pure gold.
25.37: You shall make its lamps, seven in number, and place its lamps on the branches, so as to give light in front.
25.38: Her tweezers and ashtrays will be made of pure gold.
25.39: A talent of pure gold will be used to make the candlestick with all its utensils.
25.40: "Look and follow the model shown to you on the mountain."»

26.1: «"You shall make the Tabernacle of ten curtains, you shall make them of fine twined linen, of blue, purple, scarlet and crimson, with cherubim, the work of a skilled weaver.
26.2: The length of a curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits and the width of a curtain shall be four cubits, the dimension shall be the same for all the curtains.
26.3: Five of these hangings will be joined together, the other five will also be joined together.
26.4: You will put purple laces on the edge of the tapestry finishing the first assembly and you will do the same on the edge of the tapestry finishing the second assembly.
26.5: You will make fifty laces on the first hanging and you will make fifty laces on the edge of the hanging finishing the second assembly and these laces will correspond to each other.
26.6: You shall make fifty gold clasps, with which you shall join the hangings one to another, so that the Tabernacle forms one whole.
26.7: You shall also make curtains of goat's hair to form a tent over the Tabernacle; you shall make eleven of these curtains.
26.8: The length of one curtain will be thirty cubits and the width of one curtain will be four cubits; the dimensions will be the same for all eleven curtains.
26.9: You shall join five of these curtains separately and the other six separately, and you shall fold the sixth curtain over the front of the tent.
26.10: You will put fifty laces at the edge of the tapestry finishing the first assembly and fifty more at the edge of the tapestry of the second assembly.
26.11: You will make fifty bronze staples, insert the staples into the laces and thus assemble the tent, which will form a single whole.
26.12: As for the excess portion of the tent curtains, namely half the extra curtain, it will fall back onto the rear of the Tabernacle.,
26.13: and the cubits in excess, one on one side, the other on the other, over the length of the tent curtains, shall fall back on the sides of the Tabernacle, one on one side, the other on the other, to cover it.
26.14: You shall make for the tent a covering of rams' skins dyed red and a covering of dolphins' skins over it.
26.15: You shall also make the boards for the Tabernacle, acacia wood boards, set upright.
26.16: The length of a plank will be ten cubits and the width of a plank will be one and a half cubits.
26.17: Each board will have two tenons, joined to each other; you will do the same for all the boards of the Tabernacle.
26.18: You shall make the boards for the Tabernacle: twenty boards for the south face, on the right.
26.19: You shall place under the twenty planks forty silver pedestals, two pedestals under each plank for its two tenons.
26.20: For the second side of the Tabernacle, the north side, you shall make twenty planks,
26.21: as well as their forty silver pedestals, two pedestals under each board.
26.22: You shall make six boards for the back of the Tabernacle, on the west side.
26.23: You will make two boards for the corners of the Tabernacle, at the back,
26.24: They will be doubled from the bottom, forming together a single whole up to their top, up to the first ring. So it will be for both of them; they will be placed at the two corners.
26.25: There will thus be eight boards, with their silver bases, sixteen bases, two bases under each board.
26.26: You shall make crossbeams of acacia wood, five for the planks on one side of the Tabernacle,
26.27: five crossbeams for the boards on the second side of the Tabernacle and five crossbeams for the boards on the side of the Tabernacle which forms the back, towards the west.
26.28: The middle crossbeam will extend, along the planks, from one end to the other.
26.29: You shall overlay the boards with gold and you shall make their rings of gold which are to receive the crossbeams, and you shall overlay the crossbeams with gold.
26.30: You shall erect the Tabernacle according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.
26.31: You shall make a veil of purple, violet, scarlet, and crimson yarn and fine twined linen, with cherubim woven into it: the work of a skilled weaver.
26.32: You shall hang it on four acacia wood pillars, overlaid with gold, with gold hooks, and set on four silver bases.
26.33: You shall put the veil under the clasps, and it is there, behind the veil, that you shall bring in the ark of the testimony; the veil shall be for you a separation between the holy place and the most holy place.
26.34: You shall place the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the Most Holy Place.
26.35: You shall place the table outside the veil and the lampstand opposite the table, on the south side of the Tabernacle, and you shall place the table on the north side.
26.36: You shall make for the entrance of the tent a curtain of purple, violet, scarlet, crimson, and fine twined linen, the work of which is varied in design.
26.37: You shall make five acacia wood pillars for this curtain and overlay them with gold; they shall have gold hooks, and you shall cast five bronze bases for them.«

27.1: «You shall make the altar of acacia wood; its length shall be five cubits and its width five cubits. The altar shall be square and its height shall be three cubits.
27.2: At its four corners you shall make horns that shall come out of the altar, and you shall cover it with bronze.
27.3: You shall make for the altar vessels to collect the ashes, shovels, basins, forks and censers; you shall make all these utensils of bronze.
27.4: You shall make a bronze grating for the altar, and you shall put four bronze rings on the four ends of the grating.
27.5: You will place it under the cornice of the altar, from below, and the trellis will be up to half the height of the altar.
27.6: You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, which you shall overlay with bronze.
27.7: We will pass these bars through the rings and they will be on both sides of the altar when we transport it.
27.8: You will make it hollow, out of planks; we will make it as it was shown to you on the mountain.»
27.9: «You shall make the courtyard of the Tabernacle. On the south side, on the right, there shall be curtains of finely twisted linen to form the courtyard, one hundred cubits long on one side,
27.10: with twenty columns and their twenty bronze bases, the hooks of the columns and their rods shall be of silver.
27.11: Likewise, on the north side, there will be curtains one hundred cubits long, with twenty columns and their twenty bronze bases, the hooks of the columns and their rods will be silver.
27.12: On the western side, there will be fifty cubits of curtains across the width of the courtyard, with ten columns and their ten bases.
27.13: On the eastern side, at the front, the courtyard will be fifty cubits wide,
27.14: and there will be fifteen cubits of curtains on one side of the door, with three columns and their three bases,
27.15: and fifteen cubits of curtains for the second side, with three columns and their three bases.
27.16: For the gate of the courtyard, there shall be a curtain of twenty cubits, of purple, violet, scarlet, crimson and twisted linen, with various designs, as well as four pillars with their four bases.
27.17: All the columns forming the enclosure of the forecourt will be linked by silver rods, they will have silver hooks and their bases will be of bronze.
27.18: The length of the courtyard shall be one hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits on each side and its height five cubits, the curtains shall be of twisted linen and the bases of bronze.
27.19: All the utensils used in the service of the Tabernacle, all its pegs, and all the pegs of the courtyard shall be of bronze.»
27.20: «"You shall command the children of Israel to bring you crushed olive oil for the light, to keep the lamps burning continually.
27.21: In the tent of meeting, outside the veil that is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall prepare it to be burned from evening until morning before the Lord. This is a perpetual statute for the children of Israel, throughout their generations.»

28.1: «Bring to you Aaron your brother and his sons with him from among the children of Israel, to be priests in my service: Aaron, Nadab, Abiu, Eleazar and Ithamar, sons of Aaron.
28.2: You shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, to mark his dignity and to serve as an adornment for him.
28.3: You shall speak to all the skilled men whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, and they shall make Aaron's garments, so that he may be consecrated to exercise my priesthood.
28.4: These are the garments they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, an embroidered tunic, a turban, and a sash. These are the holy garments they shall make for Aaron your brother and his sons, so that they may serve me as priests.
28.5: They will use gold, purple, scarlet, crimson, and fine linen.
28.6: They shall make the ephod of gold, purple, scarlet, crimson, and finely twisted linen, woven together in a skillful weave.
28.7: It will have two shoulder pads that will join its two ends and thus it will be joined.
28.8: The belt to fasten it by passing over it will be of the same workmanship and will be one with it: it will be of gold, purple, scarlet, crimson and twisted linen.
28.9: You shall take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel:
28.10: six of their names on one stone and the other six names on the second stone, according to the order of their births.
28.11: Just as precious stones are cut and seals are engraved on them, so you shall engrave on the two stones the names of the children of Israel and set them in gold settings.
28.12: You shall place the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the children of Israel, and Aaron shall bear their names on his two shoulders before the Lord as a memorial.
28.14: and two chains of pure gold, braided in the form of cords, and you shall attach the chains in the form of cords to the settings.
28.15: You shall make a breastplate of judgment, artistically worked; you shall make it with the same workmanship as the ephod; you shall make it of gold, purple, scarlet, crimson, and fine twined linen.
28.16: It will be square and double, its length will be one span and its width one span.
28.17: You will fit it with a setting of precious stones, four rows of precious stones. First row: a sardonyx, a topaz, an emerald,
28.18: Second row: a carbuncle, a sapphire, a diamond,
28.19: Third row: an opal, an agate, an amethyst,
28.20: Fourth row: a chrysolite, an onyx, a jasper. These stones will be set in gold rosettes.
28.21: The stones will be according to the names of the sons of Israel, twelve according to their names; they will be engraved like seals, each with its name, for the twelve tribes.
28.22: You shall make for the breastplate chains of pure gold, braided in the form of cords.
28.23: You shall make two gold rings on the breastplate and you shall put the two rings at the two ends of the breastplate.
28.24: You will pass the two gold cords through the two rings at the ends of the breastplate,
28.25: and you shall attach the two ends of the two cords to the two kittens and you shall put them on the ephod's shoulder pieces, in front.
28.26: You shall make two more gold rings, and put them on the two lower ends of the breastplate, on the inner edge applied against the ephod.
28.27: And you shall make two other gold rings, which you shall put on the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod, on the front, near its attachment, above the belt of the ephod.
28.28: The breastplate will be attached by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a purple ribbon, so that the breastplate is above the belt of the ephod and the breastplate cannot be separated from the ephod.
28.29: This is how Aaron, when he enters the sanctuary, will bear on his heart the names of the sons of Israel engraved on the breastplate of judgment, as a perpetual memorial before the Lord.
28.30: You shall put the Urim and the Thummim in the breastplate of judgment, and they shall be on Aaron’s heart when he goes in before the Lord, and so Aaron shall continually bear on his heart before the Lord the judgment of the children of Israel.
28.31: You shall make the entire robe of the ephod of purple.
28.32: There will be an opening in the middle for the head and this opening will have a woven edge all around, like the opening of a coat of arms, so that the robe will not tear.
28.33: You shall place on the lower edge pomegranates of purple, violet, scarlet, and crimson, all around the lower edge,
28.34: and golden bells in the middle of them all around: a golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate on the lower edge of the robe, all around.
28.35: Aaron will put it on to fulfill his ministry, and the sound of bells will be heard when he enters the sanctuary before the Lord and when he comes out of it, and he will not die.
28.36: You shall make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it, as one engraves on a signet: Holiness to the Lord.
28.37: You will attach it with a purple ribbon so that it is on the tiara, it will be on the front of the tiara.
28.38: It will be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron will bear the guilt of the children of Israel in the holy things they dedicate, in every kind of holy offering; it will be continually on his forehead before the Lord, so that they may find favor in the sight of the Lord.
28.39: You will make the tunic out of linen, you will make a linen tiara and you will make a belt of various colors.
28.40: For Aaron’s sons, you shall make tunics, you shall make sashes for them, and you shall make miters for them, to mark their dignity and to serve as an adornment for them.
28.41: You shall clothe Aaron, your brother, and his sons with him with these ornaments. You shall anoint them, install them, and consecrate them, so that they may be priests to serve me.
28.42: Make them linen underpants to cover their nakedness; they will go from their waist to their thighs.
28.43: Aaron and his sons shall wear them when they enter the tent of meeting, or when they approach the altar to minister in the sanctuary, so that they do not incur guilt and die. This is a perpetual statute for Aaron and his descendants after him.

29.1: Here is what you must do to consecrate them to my service as priests: Take a young bull and two rams without blemish,
29.2: unleavened breads, unleavened cakes kneaded with oil and unleavened flatbreads sprinkled with oil: you shall make all of them from fine wheat flour.
29.3: You will put them in a single basket and you will present them in the basket at the same time as you present the young bull and the two rams.
29.4: You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and you shall wash them with water.
29.5: Then, having taken the clothes, you shall dress Aaron in the tunic, the robe of the ephod, the ephod and the breastpiece, and you shall put the sash of the ephod on him.
29.6: You shall place the tiara on her head and you shall place the diadem of sanctity on the tiara.
29.7: You will take the anointing oil, pour it on his head, and anoint him.
29.8: You shall bring his sons near and clothe them with tunics.
29.9: You shall put a sash on Aaron and his sons, and you shall fasten miters on Aaron's sons. The priesthood shall belong to them by a perpetual statute, and you shall install Aaron and his sons.
29.10: You shall bring the bull before the tent of meeting, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull.
29.11: You shall slaughter the bull before the Lord at the entrance to the tent of meeting,
29.12: You shall take some of the bull's blood, put some of it with your finger on the horns of the altar, and pour out all the blood at the base of the altar.
29.13: You shall take all the fat that covers the entrails, the network of the liver and the two kidneys with the fat that surrounds them and you shall burn all of it on the altar.
29.14: But you shall burn the flesh of the bull, its hide, and its excrement by fire outside the camp: it is a sin offering.
29.15: You shall take one of the rams and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.
29.16: You shall slaughter the ram, take its blood and sprinkle it all around the altar.
29.17: You shall cut the ram into pieces and, having washed the entrails and the legs, you shall place them on the pieces and on its head,
29.18: and you shall burn the whole ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord, a pleasing aroma, a sacrifice by fire to the Lord.
29.19: You shall take the second ram and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.
29.20: You shall slaughter the ram and, having taken some of its blood, you shall put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear and on the lobe of his sons’ right ears, on the thumb of their right hands and on the big toe of their right feet, and you shall sprinkle the blood on the altar all around.
29.21: You shall take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and his garments, and on his sons and their garments with him. And so he shall be consecrated, he and his garments, and his sons and their garments with him.
29.22: You shall take the fat of the ram, the tail, the fat that covers the entrails, the network of the liver, the two kidneys and the fat that surrounds them and the right shoulder, for it is an installation ram.
29.23: You shall also take from the basket of unleavened bread placed before the Lord a loaf of bread, a cake made with oil, and a wafer.
29.24: You shall place all these things on the palms of Aaron’s hands and on the palms of his sons’ hands, and you shall wave them as a wave offering before the Lord.
29.25: Then you shall take them out of their hands and burn them on the altar on top of the burnt offering, as a pleasing aroma before the Lord: it is a sacrifice by fire to the Lord.
29.26: You shall take the breast of the ram which was used in Aaron’s installation and wave it as a wave offering before the Lord: it shall be your portion.
29.27: From the ram of installation, from what belongs to Aaron and what belongs to his sons, you shall consecrate what has been swung and what has been lifted up, namely the swung breast and the lifted shoulder:
29.28: This will be a perpetual tribute from the children of Israel to Aaron and his sons, for it is a high offering, and the children of Israel will have to take an offering from their sacrifices of thanksgiving, their offering taken for the Lord.
29.29: Aaron's sacred garments will be for his sons after him, who will wear them when they are anointed and installed.
29.30: For seven days, the one of his sons who is priest in his place will carry them, the one who enters the tent of meeting to minister in the sanctuary.
29.31: You shall take the ram of installation and boil its flesh in a holy place.
29.32: Aaron and his sons shall eat, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, the flesh of the ram and the bread that will be in the basket.
29.33: They shall eat what has been used to make atonement for them, to install and consecrate them; no foreigner shall eat of it, for it is holy.
29.34: If any of the meat from the installation and the bread remains until the next day, you shall burn the remainder and it shall not be eaten, for it is holy.
29.35: You shall do this with regard to Aaron and his sons, according to all the commandments I have given you. You shall install them for seven days.
29.36: You shall offer a young bull every day as a sin offering for atonement; you shall remove the sin from the altar by this atonement and anoint it to consecrate it.
29.37: For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it, and the altar shall be most holy, and everything that touches the altar shall be holy.
29.38: This is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old, every day, in perpetuity.
29.39: You shall offer one of these lambs in the morning and you shall offer the other lamb between the two evenings.
29.40: With the first lamb, you shall offer a tenth of an ephah of fine flour kneaded with a quarter of a hin of crushed olive oil and a libation of a quarter of a hin of wine.
29.41: You shall offer the second lamb between the two evenings, with a grain offering and a drink offering like those of the morning. It is a pleasing aroma, a burnt offering to the Lord.
29.42: a perpetual burnt offering which you must offer from age to age, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, before the Lord, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.
29.43: I will meet there with the children of Israel, and this place will be consecrated by my glory.
29.44: I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar, and I will consecrate Aaron and his sons to be priests in my service.
29.45: I will dwell among the children of Israel and I will be their God.
29.46: They will know that I, the Lord, am their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, to dwell among them, I am the Lord, their God.

30.1: You shall make an altar to burn incense on; you shall make it of acacia wood,
30.2: Its length will be one cubit and its width one cubit, it will be square and its height will be two cubits, its horns will be one piece with it.
30.3: You shall cover it with pure gold, the top, the sides all around and the horns, and you shall make a wreath of gold all around it.
30.4: You shall make for it two gold rings, below the garland, on its two edges: you shall make them on the two sides, to receive the poles which shall be used to carry it.
30.5: You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
30.6: You shall place the altar opposite the veil that is before the ark of the testimony, opposite the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with you.
30.7: Aaron will burn incense on it; he will burn it every morning when he prepares the lamps.,
30.8: And he shall burn it between the two evenings, when Aaron places the lamps on the lampstand. A perpetual incense before the Lord throughout your generations.
30.9: You shall not offer on the altar any profane incense, burnt offering, or grain offering, nor pour out any drink offering on it.
30.10: Aaron shall make atonement on the horns of the altar once a year, with the blood of the sin offering; he shall make atonement on it once a year throughout your generations. This altar shall be most holy to the Lord.»
30.11: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
30.12: «When you take a census of the children of Israel, each one shall give to the Lord a ransom for his life when they are counted, so that no plague may strike them when they are counted.
30.13: This is what all those included in the census shall give: half a shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, which is twenty gerahs, half a shekel shall be the offering raised for the Lord.
30.14: Every man included in the census, from the age of twenty years and above, shall pay the Lord's contribution.
30.15: The rich will pay no more and the poor will pay no less than half a shekel, to pay the Lord's contribution as a ransom for your souls.
30.16: You shall receive the ransom money from the Israelites and use it for the service of the tent of meeting; it shall be for the Israelites a deed before the Lord for the ransom of their souls.»
30.17: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
30.18: «You shall make a bronze basin, with a bronze base, for washing; you shall place it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and you shall put water in it.”
30.19: And Aaron and his sons will take some to wash their hands and feet.
30.20: They shall wash themselves with this water, so that they do not die, and when they enter the tent of meeting and when they approach the altar to minister, to burn a sacrifice to the Lord.
30.21: They shall wash their feet and their hands, and they shall not die. This shall be a perpetual statute for them, for Aaron and his descendants throughout their generations.»
30.22: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
30.23: «"Take, from among the best spices, five hundred shekels of virgin myrrh, half that amount, or two hundred and fifty shekels, of aromatic cinnamon, two hundred and fifty shekels of fragrant cane"
30.24: five hundred shekels of cassia, according to the sanctuary shekel, and a hin of olive oil.
30.25: You shall make of it a holy anointing oil, a perfume compounded according to the art of the perfumer: it shall be a holy anointing oil.
30.26: You shall anoint the tent of meeting and the ark of the testimony,
30.27: the table and all its utensils, the lampstand and its utensils, the altar of incense,
30.28: the altar of burnt offerings and all its utensils and the basin with its base.
30.29: You shall consecrate them and they shall be most holy; everything that touches them shall be holy.
30.30: You shall anoint Aaron and his sons and consecrate them, to serve me as priests.
30.31: You shall speak to the children of Israel, saying: This shall be the holy anointing oil for me from generation to generation.
30.32: It shall not be poured on the body of a man, nor shall you make another like it, of the same composition; it is a sacred thing and you shall regard it as a sacred thing.
30.33: Whoever composes anything like it, or places it on a foreigner, shall be cut off from his people.»
30.34: The Lord said to Moses, «Take aromatics: resin, fragrant onion, galbanum, spices and pure frankincense, they shall be in equal parts.
30.35: You shall make of it a perfume for incense, composed according to the art of the perfumer; it shall be salty, pure, and holy.
30.36: You shall grind it into a powder and put it before the testimony in the tent of meeting, where I will meet with you. It shall be most holy to you.
30.37: The perfume that you make, you shall not make for yourselves of the same composition; you shall regard it as holy to the Lord.
30.38: Anyone who makes anything like it, to smell its fragrance, will be cut off from his people.»

31.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
31.2: «Know that I have called by name Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.
31.3: I have filled him with the Spirit of God, with wisdom, with understanding, and with knowledge for all kinds of work:
31.4: to make inventions, to work with gold, silver, and bronze,
31.5: for engraving stones to be set, for carving wood and executing all kinds of work.
31.6: And behold, I have appointed Ooliab son of Ahishamech, of the tribe of Dan, to him, and I have put wisdom in the heart of every skillful man, to carry out all that I have commanded you:
31.7: the tent of meeting, the ark of the testimony, the mercy seat that is on it, and all the furnishings of the tent,
31.8: the table and its utensils, the pure gold lampstand and all its utensils, the altar of incense,
31.9: the altar of burnt offerings and all its utensils, the basin with its base,
31.10: the ceremonial garments, the sacred garments for the priest Aaron, the garments of his sons for the functions of the priesthood,
31.11: the anointing oil and the incense to be burned for the sanctuary. They will carry out all the commands I have given you.»
31.12: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
31.13: «Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: Do not fail to observe my Sabbaths, for it is a sign between me and you for all your generations, so that you may know that I, the Lord, sanctify you.
31.14: You shall observe the Sabbath, for it is holy to you. Whoever profanes it shall surely be put to death, and whoever does any work on it shall be cut off from his people.
31.15: Six days shall be worked, but the seventh day shall be a day of complete rest, holy to the Lord. Whoever does work on the Sabbath day shall be put to death.
31.16: The children of Israel shall observe the Sabbath and celebrate it, they and their descendants, as a perpetual covenant.
31.17: This will be a sign between me and the children of Israel forever, for in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day he rested from his work.»
31.18: When the Lord had finished speaking to Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of the testimony, tablets of stone, written with the finger of God.

32.1: When the people saw that Moses delayed coming down from the mountain, they gathered around Aaron and said to him, «Come, make us gods who will go before us. As for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.»
32.2: Aaron said to them, «Take off the gold rings that are in the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me.»
32.3: Everyone took off the gold rings they were wearing in their ears and brought them to Aaron.
32.4: He received them from their hands, fashioned the gold with a chisel, and made a molten calf. And they said, «Israel, these are your Gods, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.»
32.5: Having seen this, Aaron built an altar before the image and proclaimed, «Tomorrow there will be a festival in honor of the Lord.»
32.6: The next day, having risen early in the morning, they offered burnt offerings and presented peace offerings, and the people sat down to eat and drink, and then they rose up to enjoy themselves.
32.7: The Lord said to Moses, «Go downstairs, for your people whom you brought up out of the land of Egypt have behaved very badly.
32.8: They quickly turned away from the way I had commanded them; they made themselves a molten calf, they bowed down before it and offered sacrifices to it, and they said, »These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.”
32.9: The Lord said to Moses, «I see that this people is a stiff-necked people.
32.10: Now leave me alone, so that my anger may burn against them and consume them. But I will make you into a great nation.»
32.11: Moses pleaded with the Lord his God and said, «Why, Lord, should your anger burn against your people, whom you brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and a mighty hand?
32.12: Why should the Egyptians say, "It was with evil intent that he brought them out, to destroy them in the mountains and to wipe them off the face of the earth"? Turn from your fierce anger and repent of the evil you intend to do to your people.
32.13: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, your servants, to whom you swore by your own self: »I will multiply your descendants as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have promised I will give to your descendants, and they shall possess it forever.”
32.14: And the Lord relented concerning the evil he had spoken of doing to his people.
32.15: Moses returned and came down from the mountain, having in his hand the two tablets of the testimony, tablets written on both sides, they were written on both faces.
32.16: The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved on the tablets.
32.17: Joshua heard the noise of the people shouting and said to Moses, »There is a sound of war in the camp.”
32.18: Moses replied, "It is neither the sound of shouts of victory nor the sound of shouts of defeat; I hear the voice of people singing."«
32.19: When he came near the camp, he saw the calf and the dancing. And Moses' anger burned, and he threw down the tablets from his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.
32.20: And taking the calf they had made, he burned it in the fire, ground it to powder, sprinkled the powder on the water, and made the children of Israel drink it.
32.21: Moses said to Aaron, "What did this people do to you that you brought such a great sin upon them?"«
32.22: Aaron replied, «Do not let my lord’s anger burn. You yourself know that this people is prone to evil.”.
32.23: They said to me, "Make us a god who will go before us, for as for this Moses, this man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.".
32.24: I told them, 'Those who have gold should take it off.' They gave me some, I threw it into the fire, and this calf came out.»
32.25: Moses saw that the people had no restraint, because Aaron had removed all restraints, exposing them to becoming a laughingstock among their enemies.
32.26: And Moses stood at the gate of the camp and said, "Those who are for the Lord, come to me." And all the children of Levi gathered around him.
32.27: He said to them, «This is what the Lord, the God of Israel, says: »Each of you put on your sword and go back and forth through the camp from one gate to another, and each of you kill your brother, your friend, and your relative.’”
32.28: The children of Levi did as Moses commanded, and about three thousand men of the people perished that day.
32.29: Moses said, «Consecrate yourselves today to the Lord, since each of you has been against his son and father, so that he may give you a blessing today.»
32.30: The next day, Moses said to the people, «You have committed a great sin. And now I am going up to the Lord: perhaps I may obtain forgiveness for your sin.»
32.31: Moses returned to the Lord and said, «Ah, this people has committed a great sin. They have made themselves a god of gold.”.
32.32: "Forgive their sin now, or else erase me from the book you have written."»
32.33: The Lord said to Moses, «It is the one who has sinned against me whom I will blot out of my book.
32.34: Go now, lead the people to the place I told you about. Behold, my angel will go before you, but on the day of my visitation I will punish them for their sin.»
32.35: This is how the Lord struck the people, because they had made the calf that Aaron had made.

33.1: The Lord said to Moses, «Go up from here, you and the people whom you brought up out of the land of Egypt, to the land which I promised on oath to Abraham, to Isaac and to Jacob, saying, I will give it to your offspring.
33.2: I will send an angel before you, and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
33.3: Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey, but I will not go up among you, for you are a stiff-necked people, lest you be destroyed on the way.»
33.4: Upon hearing these harsh words, the people went into mourning and no one put on their ornaments.
33.5: Then the Lord said to Moses, «Tell the Israelites, »You are a stiff-necked people. If I were to go up among you for even a moment, I would destroy you. Now then, take off your ornaments so I can know what to do to you.’”
33.6: The children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments, starting at Mount Horeb.
33.7: Moses took the tent and pitched it outside the camp, some distance away; he called it the tent of meeting, and whoever sought the Lord went to the tent of meeting, which was outside the camp.
33.8: And when Moses went to the tent, all the people stood up, each one at the entrance of the tent, and they followed Moses with their eyes until he entered the tent.
33.9: As soon as Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud descended and stood at the entrance of the tent, and the Lord spoke with Moses.
33.10: All the people saw the pillar of cloud that stood at the entrance of the tent, and all the people rose up and each one bowed down at the entrance of his tent.
33.11: And the Lord spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. Then Moses returned to the camp, but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, did not leave the middle of the tent.
33.12: Moses said to the Lord, «You tell me, ‘Bring up this people,’ but you do not tell me whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have found favor in my sight.’”.
33.13: And now, if I have found favor in your eyes, make me know your ways so that I may know you and find favor in your eyes. Consider that this nation is your people.»
33.14: The Lord replied, «My face will go with you, and I will give you rest.»
33.15: Moses said, «If your face does not come, do not send us away from here.”.
33.16: How will you know that I and your people have found favor in your eyes, except by your walking with us? This will distinguish us, you and me, from all the other peoples on the face of the earth.»
33.17: The Lord said to Moses, «I will again do what you ask, for you have found favor in my sight and I know you by name.»
33.19: The Lord replied, «I will cause all my goodness to pass before you, and I will declare before you the name of the Lord; for I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy.»
33.20: The Lord said, «You will not be able to see my face, for no one may see me and live.»
33.21: The Lord said, «Here is a place near me where you may stand on the rock.
33.22: When my glory passes away, I will put you in the cleft of the rock and cover you with my hand until I have passed away.
33.23: Then I will withdraw my hand and you will see me from behind, but my face will not be seen.»

34.1: The Lord said to Moses, «Cut two tablets of stone like the first ones, and I will write on them the words that were on the first tablets, which you broke:
34.2: Be ready for tomorrow and you will go up Mount Sinai early in the morning, you will stand there before me, at the top of the mountain.
34.3: "Let no one go up with you, and let no one show themselves anywhere on the mountain, and let neither sheep nor cattle graze on that mountainside."»
34.4: So Moses cut two tablets of stone like the first ones, and getting up early, he went up Mount Sinai, as the Lord had commanded him, and he took the two tablets of stone in his hand.
34.5: The Lord descended in the cloud, stood there with him, and proclaimed the name of the Lord.
34.6: And the Lord passed before him and cried out: «Lord, Lord, God merciful and gracious, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness,
34.7: who maintains his grace to a thousand generations, and forgives wickedness, rebellion and sin, yet he does not leave them unpunished, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children and on the children's children to the third and the fourth generation.»
34.8: Immediately Moses bowed down to the ground and prostrated himself,
34.9: saying, «If I have found favor in your eyes, Lord, may the Lord walk among us, for this is a stiff-necked people; forgive our iniquities and our sins and take us as your inheritance.»
34.10: The Lord said, «Behold, I make a covenant: in the sight of all your people, I will perform wonders which have not been done in any country or nation, and all the people around you shall see the work of the Lord, for terrible are the things which I will do with you.
34.11: Pay attention to what I command you today. Behold, I will drive out before you the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
34.12: Beware of making alliances with the inhabitants of the land against which you are marching, lest they become a snare in your midst.
34.13: But you will overthrow their altars, you will break their stelae and you will cut down their sacred poles.
34.14: You shall worship no other god, for the Lord is called the Jealous One; he is a jealous God.
34.15: Therefore, do not make an alliance with the inhabitants of the land, lest, when they prostitute themselves to their gods and offer them sacrifices, they invite you and you eat of their victims,
34.16: lest you take some of their daughters for your sons, and their daughters prostitute themselves to their gods, and lead your sons to prostitute themselves to their gods as well.
34.17: You shall not make gods of molten metal.
34.18: You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread: for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I have commanded you, at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for it was in the month of Abib that you came out of Egypt.
34.19: Every firstborn belongs to me, likewise, every firstborn male of your flocks, whether ox or sheep.
34.20: You shall redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a lamb, and if you do not redeem it, you shall break its neck. You shall redeem every firstborn of your sons, and no one shall appear before me empty-handed.
34.21: Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest, even in plowing and harvest time.
34.22: You will celebrate the Feast of Weeks, the first fruits of the wheat harvest, and the harvest festival at the end of the year.
34.23: Three times a year, all the males shall present themselves before the Lord God, God of Israel.
34.24: For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders, and no one will covet your land while you go up to present yourself before the Lord your God three times a year.
34.25: You shall not offer the blood of my victim with leavened bread, nor shall the sacrifice of the Passover feast be kept overnight until morning.
34.26: You shall bring the firstfruits of your land to the house of the Lord your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.»
34.27: The Lord said to Moses, «Write these words, for it is according to these words that I am making a covenant with you and with Israel.»
34.28: Moses was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights, neither eating bread nor drinking water. And the Lord wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments.
34.29: Moses came down from Mount Sinai, Moses had in his hand the two tablets of the testimony, as he came down from the mountain and Moses did not know that the skin of his face had become radiant while he spoke with the Lord.
34.30: Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, and behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to come near him.
34.31: Moses called them, and Aaron and all the leaders of the assembly came to him, and he spoke to them.
34.32: Then all the children of Israel approached and he gave them all the commands that he had received from the Lord on Mount Sinai.
34.33: When Moses had finished speaking to them, he put a veil over his face.
34.34: When Moses went in before the Lord to speak with him, he would remove the veil until he came out, then he would come out and tell the children of Israel what he had been commanded.
34.35: The children of Israel saw the face of Moses, they saw that the skin of Moses' face was radiant, and Moses put the veil back over his face until he went in to speak with the Lord.

35.1: Moses, having summoned the whole assembly of Israel, said to them, «These are the things that the Lord has commanded to be done:
35.2: Six days you shall work, but the seventh day shall be a holy day for you, a day of complete rest in honor of the Lord. Whoever does any work on that day shall surely be put to death.
35.3: You shall not kindle a fire in any of your dwellings on the Sabbath day.»
35.4: Moses spoke to the whole assembly of the children of Israel, saying, «This is what the Lord has commanded:
35.5: Take from your possessions an offering to the Lord. Every person whose heart is willing shall bring as an offering to the Lord gold, silver, and bronze,
35.6: of violet purple, scarlet purple, crimson, fine linen, and goat hair,
35.7: rams' skins dyed red, dolphin skins, and acacia wood,
35.8: oil for the lampstand, spices for the anointing oil and for the incense,
35.9: onyx stones and other stones to be set for the ephod and for the pectoral.
35.10: Let all those among you who have ability come and carry out all that the Lord has commanded:
35.11: the Tabernacle, its tent and its covering, its rings, its frames, its crossbars, its pillars and its bases,
35.12: the ark and its bars, the mercy seat and the veil of separation,
35.13: the table with its bars and all its utensils and the breads of the proposition,
35.14: the candlestick with its utensils, lamps and oil for the candlestick,
35.15: the altar of incense and its poles, the anointing oil and the incense for burning: the curtain for the entrance to the Tabernacle,
35.16: the altar of burnt offerings, its bronze grating, its bars and all its utensils, the basin with its base,
35.17: the curtains of the forecourt, its columns, its pedestals, and the hanging of the forecourt gate,
35.18: the stakes of the Tabernacle, the stakes of the courtyard with their ropes,
35.19: the ceremonial garments for service in the sanctuary, the sacred garments for the high priest Aaron, and the garments of his sons for the functions of the priesthood.»
35.20: When the entire assembly of the children of Israel had gone out from before Moses,
35.21: All those whose hearts were inclined to it and all those whose spirits were well disposed came and brought an offering to the Lord for the building of the tent of meeting, for all its service, and for the sacred garments.
35.22: Men came as well as women, all those whose hearts were well disposed brought earrings, rings, bracelets, all kinds of gold objects, each presenting the gold offering that he had intended for the Lord.
35.23: All those who had purple, scarlet, and crimson dye, fine linen and goat hair, rams' skins dyed red, and dolphins' skins, brought them.
35.24: All those who had contributed silver and bronze offerings brought their offerings to the Lord. All those who had acacia wood in their homes for all the works intended for worship brought it.
35.25: All the women who had skill spun with their hands and brought their work: purple, scarlet, crimson, and fine linen.
35.26: All the women whose hearts were inclined to it and who had the skill, spun goat hair.
35.27: The leading men of the people brought onyx stones and other stones to be set in the ephod and the breastplate,
35.28: spices and oil for the lampstand, for the anointing oil and for the fragrant perfume.
35.29: All the children of Israel, men and women, who were willing of their hearts to contribute to any work that the Lord had commanded to be done through Moses, brought voluntary offerings to the Lord.
35.30: Moses said to the children of Israel: «Know that the Lord has chosen Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.
35.31: He filled him with the Spirit of God, with wisdom, with understanding, and with knowledge for all kinds of craftsmanship,
35.32: to make inventions, to work with gold, silver, and bronze,
35.33: for engraving stones to be set, for carving wood and executing all kinds of works of art.
35.34: He also placed in his heart the gift of teaching, just as he did in Ooliab, son of Ahishamah, of the tribe of Dan.
35.35: He filled them with intelligence to perform all kinds of sculptural and artistic work, to weave purple, scarlet, crimson, and fine linen in various designs, to carry out all kinds of work and to make inventions.

36.1: Bezalel, Ooliab, and all the intelligent men in whom the Lord has put understanding and skill to do all the work for the service of the sanctuary, shall carry it out according to all that the Lord has commanded.»
36.2: Moses summoned Bezalel, Ooliab, and all the intelligent men in whose hearts the Lord had put understanding, all those whose hearts moved them to apply themselves to this work to carry it out.
36.3: They took from Moses all the offerings that the children of Israel had brought to carry out the works intended for the service of the sanctuary, and every morning the people continued to bring Moses voluntary offerings.
36.4: Then all the skilled men who were doing all the work on the sanctuary, each leaving the work they were doing,
36.5: They came and told Moses, "The people have brought much more than is needed to carry out the work that the Lord has commanded to be done."«
36.6: Moses gave an order, and this proclamation was made in the camp: «No man or woman is to be involved any longer in the offering for the sanctuary.» And the people were forbidden to bring any more.
36.7: The prepared materials were sufficient and more than enough for all the work to be carried out.
36.8: All the skilled men among those who were working on the work made the Tabernacle of ten curtains, they made them of fine twined linen, purple, scarlet and crimson, with cherubim, the work of a skilled weaver.
36.9: The length of a curtain was twenty-eight cubits and the width of a curtain was four cubits; the dimensions were the same for all curtains.
36.10: Five of these hangings were joined together, and the other five were also joined together.
36.11: Purple laces were placed at the edge of the hanging that finished the first assembly, and the same was done at the edge of the hanging that finished the second assembly.
36.12: Fifty laces were made on the first hanging and fifty laces were made on the edge of the hanging completing the second assembly, and these laces corresponded to each other.
36.13: Fifty gold clasps were made, with which the hangings were joined together, so that the Tabernacle formed a single whole.
36.14: They made curtains of goat hair to form a tent over the Tabernacle; they made eleven of these curtains.
36.15: The length of one curtain was thirty cubits and the width of one curtain was four cubits. The dimensions were the same for all eleven curtains.
36.16: Five of these tapestries were attached separately, and the other six were kept separate.
36.17: Fifty laces were placed at the edge of the tapestry completing one assembly, and fifty laces were placed at the edge of the tapestry of the second assembly.
36.18: Fifty bronze staples were used to assemble the tent, so that it would form a single whole.
36.19: They made a covering for the tent from rams' skins dyed red and a covering from dolphins' skins on top of that.
36.20: They also made the boards for the Tabernacle, acacia wood boards, set upright.
36.21: The length of a plank was ten cubits and the width of a plank was one and a half cubits.
36.22: Each plank had two tenons, joined to each other: the same was done for all the planks of the Tabernacle.
36.23: They made the boards for the Tabernacle: twenty boards for the south face, on the right.
36.24: Under the twenty planks, forty silver pedestals were placed, two pedestals under each plank for its two tenons.
36.25: For the second side of the Tabernacle, the north side, twenty planks were made,
36.26: as well as their forty silver pedestals, two pedestals under each board.
36.27: Six boards were made for the back of the Tabernacle, on the west side.
36.28: Two boards were made for the corners of the Tabernacle, at the back,
36.29: They were doubled from the bottom, together forming a single whole up to their top, up to the first ring: so it was done for both of them, at the two corners.
36.30: There were eight boards, with their silver bases, sixteen bases, two bases under each board.
36.31: They made crossbeams of acacia wood, five for the planks on one side of the Tabernacle,
36.32: five crossbeams for the boards on the second side of the Tabernacle and five crossbeams for the boards on the side of the Tabernacle which forms the back, towards the west.
36.33: The middle crossbeam extended along the planks from one end to the other.
36.34: The planks were overlaid with gold, and their rings which received the crossbeams were made of gold, and the crossbeams were overlaid with gold.
36.35: The veil was made of purple, violet, scarlet, crimson, and finely twisted linen, with cherubim depicted on it: the work of a skilled weaver.
36.36: Four acacia pillars were made for him, overlaid with gold, with gold hooks, and four silver bases were cast for them.
36.37: They made for the entrance of the tent a curtain of purple, violet, scarlet, crimson and twisted linen, the work of a varied design.
36.38: For this curtain, five columns and their hooks were made, and their capitals and rods were covered with gold; their five bases were bronze.

37.1: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood; its length was two and a half cubits, its width one and a half cubits, and its height one and a half cubits.
37.2: He covered it with pure gold, inside and out, and he made a golden wreath all around it.
37.3: He cast four gold rings for her, which he placed on her four feet, two rings on one side and two rings on the other.
37.4: He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
37.5: He passed the poles through the rings on the sides of the ark, to carry it.
37.6: He made a mercy seat of pure gold, its length was two and a half cubits, its width one and a half cubits.
37.7: He made two cherubim of gold, he made them of hammered gold, at the two ends of the mercy seat,
37.8: one cherub at one end and one cherub at the other end, he made the cherubim coming out of the mercy seat at its two ends.
37.9: The cherubim had their wings spread upwards, covering the mercy seat with their wings and facing each other, the faces of the cherubim were turned towards the mercy seat.
37.10: He made the table of acacia wood; its length was two cubits, its width one cubit, and its height one and a half cubits.
37.11: He adorned it with pure gold and placed a golden wreath all around it.
37.12: He made a frame around it, a palm's width wide, and he made a gold garland on the frame all around.
37.13: He cast four gold rings for the table and put the rings on the four corners, which are at its four feet.
37.14: The rings were near the frame to receive the bars that would support the table.
37.15: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold; they were used to carry the table.
37.16: He made the utensils that were to be placed on the table, its dishes, its goblets, its cups and its ewers for serving libations, he made them of pure gold.
37.17: He made the lampstand of pure gold, he made the lampstand of hammered gold, with its base and its stem, its cups, its buds and its flowers were of one piece.
37.18: Six branches came out of its sides, three branches from the lampstand on one side and three branches from the lampstand on the other side.
37.19: On the first branch there were three almond blossom calyxes, bud and flower, and on the second branch three almond blossom calyxes, bud and flower; the same was true for the six branches coming from the candelabra.
37.20: On the stem of the candlestick were four chalices, in almond blossoms, with their buds and flowers.
37.21: There was a button under the first two branches coming out of the stem of the candlestick, a button under the next two branches coming out of the stem of the candlestick and a button under the last two branches coming out of the stem of the candlestick, according to the six branches coming out of the candlestick.
37.22: These buttons and branches were of the same piece as the candlestick, the whole thing was a mass of beaten gold, of pure gold.
37.23: He made his lamps, seven in number, and his tongs and lampstands, of pure gold.
37.24: A talent of pure gold was used to make the candlestick with all its utensils.
37.25: He made the altar of incense of acacia wood; its length was one cubit and its width one cubit, it was square and its height was two cubits, its horns were one piece with it.
37.26: He covered it with pure gold, the top, the sides all around and the horns, and he made a wreath of gold all around it.
37.27: He made for it two gold rings, below its garland, on its two edges, he made them on the two sides, to receive the poles which were used to carry it.
37.28: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
37.29: He made the oil for holy anointing and the perfume for incense, composed according to the art of the perfumer.

38.1: He made the altar of burnt offerings of acacia wood; its length was five cubits and its width five cubits, it was square and its height was three cubits.
38.2: At its four corners he made horns that came out of the altar, and he clothed it with bronze.
38.3: He made all the utensils for the altar, the containers for the ashes, the shovels, the basins, the forks and the censers; he made all these utensils of bronze.
38.4: He made a bronze grille in the shape of a trellis for the altar, and placed it under the cornice of the altar, from below, up to half the height.
38.5: He cast four rings, which he placed at the four corners of the bronze grid, to receive the bars.
38.6: He made the bars from acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
38.7: He passed the bars through the rings, on the sides of the altar, so that they could be used to carry it. He made it hollow, out of planks.
38.8: He made the bronze tub and its bronze base, with the mirrors of the women who gathered at the entrance of the meeting tent.
38.9: He made the courtyard. On the south side, to the right, the curtains of the courtyard, made of twisted linen, were one hundred cubits long.,
38.10: with twenty columns and their twenty bronze bases, the hooks of the columns and their rods were silver.
38.11: On the north side, the curtains were one hundred cubits long with twenty columns and their twenty bronze bases; the hooks of the columns and their rods were silver.
38.12: On the western side, the curtains were fifty cubits long, with ten columns and their ten bases.
38.13: On the eastern side, in front, there were fifty cubits:
38.14: and there were fifteen cubits of curtains on one side of the door, with three columns and their three bases,
38.15: and for the second side, on one side of the gate of the courtyard as on the other, fifteen cubits of curtains with three columns and their three bases.
38.16: All the curtains forming the enclosure of the forecourt were made of twisted linen.
38.17: The bases for the columns were bronze, the column hooks and their rods were silver, and their capitals were silver-plated. All the columns in the courtyard were connected by silver rods.
38.18: The curtain for the gate of the courtyard was a work of varied design, in purple, scarlet, crimson, and finely twisted linen; its length was twenty cubits and its height five cubits, like the width of the curtains of the courtyard.,
38.19: Its four columns and their four bases were of bronze, the hooks and their rods of silver, and their capitals covered in silver.
38.20: All the stakes for the Tabernacle and for the enclosure of the courtyard were made of bronze.
38.21: This is the account of the things that were used for the Tabernacle. The Tabernacle of the Testimony, an account drawn up by the Levites at the command of Moses and under the direction of Ithamar, son of Aaron the high priest.
38.22: Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, did everything that the Lord had commanded Moses,
38.23: He had as his assistant Ooliab, son of Ahishamah, of the tribe of Dan, skilled in engraving, in design, in weaving in various patterns purple, scarlet, crimson and fine linen.
38.24: The total amount of gold used in the work, for all the work of the sanctuary, gold which was the product of the offerings: twenty-nine talents and seven hundred and thirty shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
38.25: The money of those of the assembly who were counted amounted to one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
38.26: It was one beka per head, half a shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, for each man included in the census, from the age of twenty years and above, that is for six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty men.
38.27: The one hundred talents of silver were used to melt the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil, one hundred bases for the one hundred talents, one talent per base.
38.28: And with the one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels, they made the hooks for the columns, they covered the capitals and they joined them with rods.
38.29: The bronze for the offerings amounted to seventy talents and two thousand four hundred shekels.
38.30: They made from these the bases for the entrance to the tent of meeting, the bronze altar with its bronze grid, and all the altar utensils.,
38.31: the bases of the enclosure of the courtyard and the bases of the gate of the courtyard and all the stakes of the Tabernacle and all the stakes of the enclosure of the courtyard.

39.1: With purple, violet, scarlet, and crimson, they made the ceremonial garments for the service in the sanctuary and they made the sacred garments for Aaron, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.2: They made the ephod of gold, violet purple, scarlet purple, crimson, and twisted linen.
39.3: They spread the gold into sheets and cut them into threads, which they interlaced with purple, scarlet, crimson and fine linen, with varied designs.
39.4: Shoulder pads were made to join it, and thus it was joined at both ends.
39.5: The belt for attaching the ephod by passing over it was one piece with it and was of the same workmanship; it was of gold, purple, scarlet, crimson, and finely twisted linen, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.6: They made onyx stones set in gold settings, on which they engraved the names of the sons of Israel, like engraving seals.
39.7: They were placed on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the children of Israel, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.8: They made the breastplate, artfully worked, with the same workmanship as the ephod, of gold, purple, scarlet, crimson, and twisted linen.
39.9: It was square, the pectoral was made double, its length was a span and its width a span, it was double.
39.10: It is adorned with four rows of stones: a row of sardonyx, topaz, and emerald: first row,
39.11: Second row: a carbuncle, a sapphire, a diamond,
39.12: Third row: an opal, an agate, an amethyst,
39.13: Fourth row: a chrysolite, an onyx, a jasper. These stones were surrounded by gold rosettes in their settings.
39.14: The stones were according to the names of the sons of Israel, twelve according to their names; they were engraved like seals, each with its name, for the twelve tribes.
39.15: They made for the breastplate chains of pure gold, braided in the form of cords.
39.16: They made two gold settings and two gold rings and put the two rings at the two ends of the breastplate.
39.17: The two gold cords were passed through the two rings at the ends of the pectoral,
39.18: and the two ends of the two cords were attached to the two kittens and placed on the ephod's shoulder pieces, in front.
39.19: Two more gold rings were made, which were placed at the two lower ends of the breastplate, on the inner edge applied against the ephod.
39.20: Two more gold rings were made, one of which was placed at the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod on the front, near the fastening, above the belt of the ephod.
39.21: The breastplate was fastened by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a purple ribbon, so that the breastplate was above the belt of the ephod and the breastplate could not be separated from the ephod, as the Lord commanded Moses.
39.22: The robe of the ephod was made, the work of the weaver, entirely of purple.
39.23: In the middle of the ephod's robe, there was an opening similar to that of a coat of arms, and this opening had a woven edge all around, so that the robe would not tear.
39.24: They placed on the lower edge of the robe pomegranates of violet, scarlet, crimson, and twisted linen,
39.25: They made little bells of pure gold and placed these bells in the middle of the pomegranates, on the lower edge of the robe all around, in the middle of the pomegranates:
