39.26: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, on the edge of the robe all around, for service, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.27: They made linen tunics, the work of a weaver, for Aaron and his sons,
39.28: the linen tiara and linen miters used as adornment, the white breeches of twisted linen,
39.29: the belt of twisted linen, in purple, violet, scarlet and crimson, damask, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.30: They made the blade of pure gold, a sacred diadem, and engraved on it, as one engraves on a seal: Holiness to the Lord.
39.31: It was fastened with a purple ribbon to be placed on the tiara, at the top, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.32: Thus was completed all the work of the Tabernacle, of the tent of meeting, and the children of Israel carried out everything according to what the Lord had commanded Moses, they did so.
39.33: The Tabernacle was presented to Moses, the tent and all its furnishings, its clasps, its boards, its crossbars, its pillars and its bases,
39.34: the red-dyed ramskin covering, the dolphin skin covering, and the separating veil,
39.35: the ark of the testimony with its bars and the mercy seat,
39.36: the table with all its utensils and the breads of the suggestion,
39.37: the pure gold lampstand, its lamps, the lamps to be stored in it, all its utensils, and the oil for the lamp,
39.38: the golden altar, the anointing oil and the incense, as well as the curtain for the entrance to the tent,
39.39: the bronze altar, its bronze grille, its bars and all its utensils, the basin with its base, the curtains of the courtyard, its columns, its pedestals,
39.40: the curtain for the gate of the courtyard, its cords and stakes, and all the utensils for the service of the Tabernacle, for the tent of meeting
39.41: the ceremonial garments for the service of the sanctuary, the sacred garments for the high priest Aaron and the garments of his sons for the functions of the priesthood.
39.42: The children of Israel had done all this work in accordance with everything that the Lord had commanded Moses.
39.43: Moses examined all the work, and behold, they had carried it out, they had done it as the Lord had commanded. And Moses blessed them.

40.1: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
40.2: «On the first day of the first month, you shall set up the Tabernacle, the tent of meeting.
40.3: You shall place the ark of the testimony there and cover the ark with the veil.
40.4: You will bring the table and arrange what is to be placed on it. You will bring the lampstand and place its lamps on it.
40.5: You shall place the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony and you shall put the veil at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
40.6: You shall place the altar of burnt offerings before the entrance of the Tabernacle, the tent of meeting.
40.7: You shall place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it.
40.8: You shall set up the courtyard around it and put up the curtain at the gate of the courtyard.
40.9: «Take the anointing oil and anoint the Tabernacle and everything in it, and consecrate it with all its utensils, and it shall be holy.
40.10: You shall anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, you shall consecrate the altar, and the altar shall be most holy.
40.11: You shall anoint the basin with its base and consecrate it.
40.12: «"You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
40.13: Then you shall clothe Aaron with the holy garments, anoint him, and consecrate him, and he shall be a priest in my service.
40.14: You shall bring his sons near and, having clothed them in tunics,
40.15: You shall anoint them as you anointed their father, and they shall be priests in my service. This anointing shall confer upon them a priesthood forever among their descendants.»
40.16: Moses did everything that the Lord had commanded him; he did so.
40.17: On the first day of the first month of the second year, the Tabernacle was set up.
40.18: Moses erected the Tabernacle, he laid its bases, he placed its boards and crossbeams, and set up its pillars.
40.19: He spread the tent over the Tabernacle and put the tent covering over it, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.20: He took the testimony and placed it in the ark, he put the poles in the ark and placed the mercy seat on top of the ark.
40.21: He brought the ark into the Tabernacle and, having put on the veil of separation, he covered the ark of the testimony, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.22: He placed the table in the tent of meeting, on the north side of the Tabernacle, outside the veil,
40.23: and he arranged the loaves before the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.24: He placed the lampstand in the tent of meeting, opposite the table, on the south side of the Tabernacle,
40.25: and he placed the lamps there before the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.26: He placed the golden altar in the tent of meeting, before the veil
40.27: and he burned incense there, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.28: He placed the curtain at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
40.29: He placed the altar of burnt offerings at the entrance of the Tabernacle, the tent of meeting, and offered the burnt offering and the grain offering there, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.30: He placed the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for ablutions,
40.31: Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and feet there.
40.32: When they entered the tent of meeting and approached the altar, they washed themselves, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
40.33: He set up the courtyard around the Tabernacle and the altar, and he hung the curtain at the gate of the courtyard. Thus Moses completed this work.
40.34: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the Lord filled the Tabernacle.
40.35: And Moses could no longer enter the tent of meeting, because the cloud remained over it and the glory of the Lord filled the Tabernacle.
40.36: For as long as their journeys lasted, the children of Israel would set out whenever the cloud rose above the Tabernacle,
40.37: And if the cloud did not rise, they did not leave, until the day it rose.
40.38: For the cloud of the Lord rested over the Tabernacle by day, and there was fire in the cloud by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, as long as they marched.
